content:
{
  "title": "Adversarial Prompt Diversification: Stochastic Ensemble Prompting for Robust LLM Defense",
  "problem": "Current LLMs remain susceptible to adversarial attacks and jailbreaks despite prompt engineering and fine-tuning. There is a lack of dynamic prompting methods that can proactively disrupt and detect adversarial prompt patterns in real-time.",
  "motivation": "Most defenses rely on static prompts or post-hoc detection, which adversaries can eventually circumvent by reverse engineering. As LLM deployment accelerates, robust and adaptive prompting strategies are urgently needed to reduce model vulnerability and strengthen guardrails without degrading utility.",
  "method": "We propose Adversarial Prompt Diversification (APD), a framework that dynamically samples from a pool of semantically equivalent but structurally diverse prompts at inference. For each user query, APD stochastically applies different rephrasings, task decompositions, and context orderings (using automated paraphrase models and context randomization) and aggregates the model’s responses. Discrepancies in output consistency or abnormal behavior across prompt variants trigger automated risk flags. This method proactively disrupts pattern-matching jailbreaks and enables on-the-fly detection of adversarial intent.",
  "experiment_plan": "We evaluate APD on public jailbreak/adversarial prompt suites (e.g., JailbreakBench, AdvBench), comparing against static prompt baselines and AdaShield. Metrics include jailbreak success rate, false positive rate, task utility, and defense latency. Ablation studies assess the contribution of paraphrasing and context randomization components."
}
